prerequisites: An active Stigg account with access to the Stigg dashboard.
A Stigg Full access (server-side) API key, copied from the dashboard under Integrations > API keys for the environment you want to read from. Keys are environment-specific.
The api_key value available to supply when configuring the connector (this is the connector's only required field).

authMethods.name: API key (X-API-KEY header)
authMethods.summary: Stigg authenticates requests with the Full access (server-side) API key passed in the X-API-KEY request header. This maps to the connector's required api_key secret field. The key should be kept server-side and never exposed to clients.

setupSteps.title: Get your Full access API key
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the Stigg dashboard and open Integrations > API keys. Copy the Full access key for the environment you want to sync. Stigg API keys are scoped per environment, so make sure you take the key for the correct (for example production) environment.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API key to the connector
setupSteps.body: Configure the connector and set the required api_key field to the Full access key you copied. Stigg uses this value in the X-API-KEY header to authenticate against its GraphQL API at https://api.stigg.io/graphql.
